critique.summary: Meta announced that starting in 2026, the Horizon Worlds VR app will be removed from Meta Quest devices, transitioning Horizon Worlds to a mobile-only platform. Several Horizon Worlds features and perks will be discontinued or moved, and users will no longer be able to access Horizon Worlds in VR after June 15, 2026.
mainPoints: Horizon Worlds and Events will be removed from the Meta Quest Store by March 31, 2026.
Horizon Worlds VR app will be removed from Quest devices after June 15, 2026.
Users can still access mobile-optimized Horizon Worlds via the Meta Horizon mobile app after June 15, 2026.
Horizon Hyperscape Capture (Beta) will no longer support sharing and co-experiencing Hyperscapes after March 24, 2026.
Horizon-specific perks in Meta Horizon Plus subscriptions will be removed by March 31, 2026, but core gaming benefits remain.
Meta is continuing to update Quest with new features unrelated to Horizon Worlds.
critique.truthfulness: The claims in the article are consistent with the official announcement from Meta's own community forums and support channels, which confirm the planned discontinuation of Horizon Worlds on Meta Quest VR devices and the shift to a mobile-only experience. The timeline and details about feature removals and app availability match Meta's published updates. User comments reflect a range of reactions but do not contradict the factual content of the announcement.
considerations: The article does not discuss the potential impact on the VR community and creators who rely on Horizon Worlds for social interaction and content creation.
There is no mention of alternative VR platforms or solutions for displaced users and creators.
The article does not provide data or rationale from Meta explaining the business or technical reasons behind this strategic shift.
User experience issues with the mobile version of Horizon Worlds are noted in comments but not addressed by Meta in the announcement.

comments_critique.summary: The discussion centers around Meta's decision to discontinue Horizon Worlds on Meta Quest, with commenters expressing disappointment over wasted investment, poor strategic direction, and leadership issues. Many highlight the company's mismanagement and question the viability of the metaverse vision, while some speculate on layoffs and the future of VR at Meta.

comments.author: atrus
comments.text:
Despite the shock at the amount of money meta spent on their version of the metaverse, I don't think they spent nearly enough to accomplish their vision.

Meta, to the detriment of the market, tried too early in the VR lifecycle to own the market. They basically tried to become the iPhone and Apple in the year 1990.

Tell me, do you believe any singular company in the year 1990, with 100B to burn, would be able to create the iPhone, in any of its varations? Absolutely not, there too much research, too much to invent, too much to program and not nearly enough talent and money for one company to manage.
comments.analysis: This comment provides a nuanced perspective on the timing and scale of Meta's investment, accurately noting the challenges of pioneering a complex new market.

comments.author: t-writescode
comments.text:
They were trying to compete with an existing, VERY good couple of alternatives, and the people most actually likely to use that product were already on those services.

It was a losing play that didn’t know what market it was actually entering.
comments.analysis: This comment correctly identifies market competition and user base loyalty as key factors in Horizon Worlds' failure to gain traction.

comments.author: paxys
comments.text: It’s wild how much mismanagement Zuck is able to get away with just because he controls majority voting shares in Meta. At this point all of the missteps by the company in AR/VR, AI and everything else can directly be traced back to him. Half-baked vision, massive spending and no accountability. The company desperately needs a Sundar/Satya type leader.
comments.analysis: While somewhat emotional, this comment insightfully links leadership issues to strategic failures, a common critique supported by industry analysis.

comments.author: tyleo
comments.text:
Interesting, cutting way back in the product they renamed the whole company for.

They feel a bit directionless to me. They are still making money but even their AI attempt feels half hearted. I think they are really trying but I’m not sure they can build the engineering muscle to move in new areas with the brand damage they’ve sustained.
comments.analysis: This comment reflects a reasonable interpretation of Meta's challenges but is somewhat speculative about internal capabilities.

comments.author: xd1936
comments.text: Unbelievable. They re-architected the whole operating system around this stupid app. They discontinued their previous homescreen environments in favor of trying to promote Horizon Worlds, only to discontinue the blasted thing anyway? After all of those millions of dollars spent trying to make virtual events happen?
comments.analysis: The comment accurately notes the significant investment and abrupt discontinuation, though it expresses frustration in a somewhat emotional tone.

comments.author: drivebyhooting
comments.text: When will horizon worlds be axed entirely? It has something like fewer than 10,000 active users at any given time.
comments.analysis: This user provides a plausible estimate of low user engagement, which aligns with public reports on Horizon Worlds' limited adoption.

comments.author: cheriot
comments.text:
Condolences to those that about to be laid off

Context: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/14/meta-reportedly-considerin...
comments.analysis: This comment is empathetic and references credible external reporting, though it does not add technical analysis.

comments.author: jesse_dot_id
comments.text:
I never understood why they were trying to recreate real life social interactions in VR, because it's worse by default, and the majority of the nerds who buy this tech are probably trying to escape that on some level. I know that any time I went into Meta Horizon Worlds, I didn't want to hear 95% of the people I heard talking.

What I do use VR for is Bigscreen VR nearly every night to watch stuff with my friends. Scrolling through reels in a movie theater is pretty fun and even though I never do it solo on my phone, I will sit there for like 3-4 hours in VR enjoying communal brain rot.

Perhaps they should focus on things like that instead of gimmicks that nobody cares about. For example, I have never once played a game in VR that didn't force me to sit or stand in a specific position, meaning to play it, I have to go out of my way to do so.
comments.analysis: This personal usage insight highlights a mismatch between user preferences and Meta's product focus, offering constructive feedback.

comments.author: ralusek
comments.text: Maybe I'm just naive but I don't really understand discontinuing things like this. Like, unless there are like 100 people using this, how can it not be possible to just leave this running at like 0.5% of its former capacity. Just leave up like 1 server, collapse all of the DBs into one, and let these few autists have their stuff.
comments.analysis: This comment raises a practical point about cost management and user retention, suggesting a less drastic shutdown approach.

comments.author: XCSme
comments.text:
I have a Quest 2, I only use it to play Eleven Table Tennis VR.

I don't understand what Horizon Worlds is or the other thing mentioned.
comments.analysis: This comment reflects a typical user perspective, indicating limited awareness or interest in Horizon Worlds, supporting low adoption claims.
